# Comparing using k‑mers {#sec-intro-kmers}

Intro: what information are we okay to give up in order to speed up comparisons / gain space

## Coarse-grained comparisons with /kmers

- alternative to bp-level comparison of full-text indices
- naturally lead to data structures based on lookup tables (for small kmers) or hash maps (for bigger ones)
- in our applications, we mostly use $k \in [20, 70]$, leading to pretty sparse /kmer sets
- notion of /kmer spectra, characterizing a sequence
- when coupled with abundance, it can help to identify erroneous /kmers or different haplotypes

## De Bruijn graph representation

- definition, sequence as path
- application to error correction and assembly
- compact representations: unitigs, SPSS

## Using /kmers for scalable data structures

- petabase-scale indexes
- colored compacted dBGs
